Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 1-3: Temp: 20-21 (Ambient/Low) Pressure: 1 (Baseline) Vibration: 0 (Off/Idle) Flow: 0 (None) Tool Pos: 0 (Home/Rest) Energy: 0.40-0.50 (Very low/Standby) Interpretation: Machine is on but not actively processing. Idle/Standby state. Events 4-6: Temp: 50 -> 55 -> 58 (Rising fast, significant heat) Pressure: 1 -> 1.5 (Slight increase) Vibration: 30 -> 35 -> 38 (High and increasing) Flow: 5 -> 5 -> 5 (Steady, active material movement) Tool Pos: 5 -> 7 -> 10 (Moving through material) Energy: 2.00 -> 2.50 -> 3.00 (High and increasing) Interpretation: Active, aggressive process. High vibration and high material flow suggest a subtractive process or shaping. Rising temperature and tool position moving indicate a tool engaging with material. This strongly matches Cutting Metal (machining/milling). Events 7-8: Temp: 40 -> 40 (Moderate, dropping from cutting) Pressure: 2 -> 2 (Slightly elevated) Vibration: 10 -> 10 (Low to moderate) Flow: 2 -> 2 (Steady, lower flow) Tool Pos: 15 -> 15 (Stationary/Reached position) Energy: 2.00 -> 2.00 (Moderate, steady) Interpretation: Stable, precise phase. Lower vibration than cutting, tool position is static, moderate energy. Material flow is present but lower. This suggests putting things together without high impact. Matches Assembling Parts (fitting, pressing, or joining). Event 9: Temp: 80 (Spikes very high!) Pressure: 2 (Elevated) Vibration: 50 (Spikes very high!) Flow: 0 (No material flowing in) Tool Pos: 15 (Stationary) Energy: 5.00 (Highest energy spike) Interpretation: Extreme heat, extreme energy, stationary tool position, no material inflow (the material is already there from assembling), high vibration (arc/flame instability). This is a classic signature of Welding (joining parts using intense heat).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Events 10-12: Temp: 30 -> 25 -> 25 (Cooling down) Pressure: 1 -> 1 (Normal) Vibration: 1 -> 5 -> 5 (Very low) Flow: 0 -> 1 -> 1 (Low/slight flow - maybe packaging material or conveyor) Tool Pos: 15 -> 20 -> 20 (Shifting slightly, maybe pushing) Energy: 1.00 -> 1.50 -> 1.60 (Low to moderate) Interpretation: Post-welding cooldown or final steps. Low temp, low vibration. The shift in tool position and slight flow could be scanning/inspecting and then moving to the next stage. Since we need to map to Quality Inspection or Packaging, lets split 10-12. Event 10: Temp dropping to 30, Vibration 1, Flow 0, Energy 1.00. Could be Quality Inspection (cooling, sensors active, low energy). Wait, lets look closer. Events 10: Inspection (just looking/measuring, no flow, low energy). Events 11-12: Flow starts (1), Tool moves (20), Energy goes up slightly (1.50-1.60). This looks like Packaging (wrapping/boxing, moving the finished product out).

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 13-15: Temp: 20-21 (Ambient) Pressure: 1 (Baseline) Vibration: 0 (Off) Flow: 0 (None) Tool Pos: 0 (Home) Energy: 0.40-0.50 (Standby) Interpretation: Cycle complete. Returning to Idle/Standby.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize and Map to Labels: Phase 1 (Events 1-3, 13-15): Idle / Standby Phase 2 (Events 4-6): Cutting Metal Phase 3 (Events 7-8): Assembling Parts Phase 4 (Event 9): Welding Phase 5 (Event 10): Quality Inspection Phase 6 (Events 11-12): Packaging

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft the Rationale: Idle/Standby: Temp, vibration, flow, tool pos at baseline zero. Minimal energy just to keep the machine controller running. Cutting Metal: High vibration (blade/teeth engaging), rising temperature (friction), positive material flow (chips/cutting fluid or feed rate), moving tool position (advancing the cut), high energy. Assembling Parts: Moderate and stable readings. Vibration drops, tool position stops advancing (parts mated), steady moderate energy and flow (mechanical joining/pressing). Welding: Extreme temperature spike (molten metal), extreme energy spike (electrical arc or laser), very high vibration (arc instability/spatter), zero flow (using existing parts), tool position stationary. Quality Inspection: Rapid drop in temperature and vibration (post-weld). Low energy, zero flow, stationary tool (optical/tactile sensors measuring the weld). Packaging: Slight increase in flow (packaging material introduced), tool position shifts (ejecting or sealing), moderate energy.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Double-check the event numbers and data points to ensure accuracy in the explanation. (e.g., Event 9 Temp is 80, Energy is 5.0; Events 4-6 Temp is 50-58, Vib is 30-38). Matches perfectly.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Refine the Output: Make sure the structure directly answers the prompt: Reviewing the log (briefly acknowledge the method). Identify distinct phases/groupings. Assign intuitive labels. Provide rationale for each. Output the labeled activities and explanation. Ensure tone is analytical and process-mining oriented. Final Polish: Organize with clear headings, bullet points, and a summarizing table for readability. (Self-correction: The prompt asks to output labeled activities along with an explanation. A table mapping Event # to Activity Name followed by detailed rationales would be excellent). Lets go with that structure.
